solute.description: This molecule features a bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane (norbornane) skeleton substituted with three methyl groups: two geminal methyls on the C7 bridge and one at the C1 bridgehead. A single secondary hydroxyl group is positioned at the C2 carbon, identifying the structure as a saturated bicyclic terpenoid alcohol (specifically borneol or isoborneol). The molecule is characterized by a compact, rigid, and bulky hydrophobic hydrocarbon cage, which results in low overall polarity despite the localized polar functional group. It possesses a single site for hydrogen bonding, acting as both a donor and an acceptor through the oxygen atom of the hydroxyl group.

solvent.description: This molecule consists of a central benzene ring substituted at the 1 and 4 positions (para-substitution) with two distinct alkyl groups. Specifically, it features a methyl group and an isopropyl group located directly opposite each other on the aromatic core. The structure is entirely hydrocarbon-based, rendering it nonpolar and devoid of any hydrogen bond donors or acceptors. Its distinctive character arises from the combination of a rigid planar aromatic ring with branched and linear aliphatic side chains, resulting in a relatively small, hydrophobic molecule.
